nodes.label: Immutable Image Deployment
nodes.rationale: Release images are built, scanned, published, resolved to registry digests, signed and then supplied to Terraform so the deployed artifact is the verified artifact.

nodes.label: ECS Fargate Deployment Choice
nodes.rationale: ECS/Fargate was selected over EKS and Argo CD because it provides a complete, manageable deployment path within the four-hour exercise constraint.

nodes.label: Public GHCR Registry Choice
nodes.rationale: Public GHCR keeps source, CI and artifacts in GitHub; ECS can pull public images without runtime registry credentials.

nodes.label: OIDC Instead of Static AWS Keys
nodes.rationale: OIDC supplies short-lived credentials and reduces long-lived secret storage, leakage risk and rotation burden.
nodes.norm_label: oidc instead of static aws keys

nodes.label: Sonar Trivy Dependabot Complementarity
nodes.rationale: SonarQube assesses source-code quality, Trivy scans repository and image security, and Dependabot drives recurring dependency updates.

nodes.label: Terraform Bootstrap and Production Isolation
nodes.rationale: Bootstrap is separate from production so it can create the remote state backend and GitHub OIDC role before production infrastructure is initialized.

nodes.label: Cost-Conscious AWS Platform
nodes.rationale: A public VPC, one Fargate task and no NAT Gateway reduce demonstration cost while security groups retain ALB-only task ingress.
